entries.name: Sefa — young ex-Crown guard who walked off her post and fled the Reach at your side; owes you her life, looking to you for what comes next
The Moor — the famished thing out of the waking Heath that broke the Reach; Moor's Hunger given a body, and a sign of worse beneath the heather
The howe-dead — restless dead stirring in the barrows of the eastern heath as the Working's strain cracks their seals
Reeve Godric — the Crown's man who held the Reach; spent himself at the breach to buy the pen its seconds, fate unknown

The nurse & the child — two drift-come you pulled off the howe-field; now flee east with you and Sefa. The rest of the Reach's survivors scattered into the waking barrow-heath in the night, most likely lost
The Stonebay buyer — Ranulf's agent who fled the siege; still pricing strangers, and you're walking toward his master's markets
Duke Ranulf of Stonebay — lord of the eastern duchy that traffics in people; wants the drift-come
Count Edmund — the hardline lord who would put the drift-come to the sword; a standing power to the west

Bron — Stanwic's wayhouse-keeper; tried to feed the drift-come to the heath, then to seize the town's defense — beaten both times (the mob, then the forge). Twice-shamed, cold, committed: a quiet, vengeful enemy now, not a loud one

Wystan — Stanwic's smith; cagey and circuitous, but he saw Shake work real steel and respects it. Granted forge + smith's-tools access (the road to rebuilding Samosa). A possible ally if handled right
